skill.name: use-skills
skill.description: Helps choose relevant installed skills for complex requests and briefly shows the working set before answering.
skill.summary: Coordinate multiple Claude skills to produce unified, high-quality responses for multi-domain tasks.
skill.author: CyrusSE

security_audit.summary: This skill is pure markdown documentation providing skill coordination guidance. The static scanner flagged backtick characters as potential command execution, but these are false positives from inline code syntax in markdown (skill names like `$use-skills`, `All related`, `Recommended`). No executable code, network requests, or malicious patterns detected. This is a documentation-only skill that helps coordinate other skills.

content.user_title: Coordinate Multiple Skills for Complex Tasks
content.value_statement: When requests span multiple domains like planning, implementation, and review, this skill helps select the right combination of installed skills and unifies their guidance into one coherent output.
actual_capabilities: Analyze user requests to identify which installed skills apply
Select and coordinate multiple skills for unified responses
Present skill selection in three modes: All related, Recommended, Restricted
Read and integrate guidance from selected skill files
Handle conflicts when skills point in different directions
Produce consolidated output combining multiple skill perspectives
limitations: Only works with skills that are already installed in the system
Cannot create or modify skills, only coordinates existing ones
Requires user interaction to confirm skill selection mode

use_cases.title: Multi-Domain Development Tasks
use_cases.description: When a request involves planning, implementation, testing, and documentation, this skill picks the relevant domain skills and coordinates their guidance into one unified plan.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers working on complex features

use_cases.title: Cross-Functional Writing Projects
use_cases.description: For tasks like writing a technical blog post that needs structure, clarity review, and code examples, this skill selects writing, review, and code skills and merges their standards.

use_cases.title: Comprehensive Code Review
use_cases.description: When reviewing code changes that impact multiple areas like security, performance, and style, this skill activates the relevant review skills and synthesizes their findings.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Skill Invocation
prompt_templates.scenario: Invoke the skill coordination helper when starting a complex multi-domain task

prompt_templates.title: Explicit Mode Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use restricted mode with only the strongest skills for this refactoring task.
prompt_templates.scenario: Request a specific coordination mode for focused skill selection

prompt_templates.title: Full Scope Request
prompt_templates.prompt: This task spans planning, implementation, testing, and documentation. Use the best combination of skills.
prompt_templates.scenario: Indicate a task needs comprehensive skill coverage

prompt_templates.title: Conflict Resolution Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code and write tests for it. Coordinate the review and testing skills.
prompt_templates.scenario: When two skills might conflict, ask for unified direction

output_examples.input: Turn this feature idea into an implementation plan with tests and documentation.
output_examples.output:
Mode: Recommended
Using: use-skills, planning, testing, documentation
For: Feature implementation with test coverage and docs

output_examples.input: Use all available skills related to this code review.
output_examples.output:
Mode: All related
Using: use-skills, security-review, performance, style-guide, testing
For: Comprehensive code review with multiple quality dimensions

best_practices: Start with $use-skills when your request touches multiple domains
Choose the mode that matches your need for breadth versus focus
Trust the skill coordination when you need unified output from multiple perspectives
anti_patterns: Do not invoke this skill for single-domain requests that one skill can handle
Do not skip mode selection when prompted, as it determines skill scope
Do not manually list skills when the coordination skill can select them

faq.question: What does the use-skills skill actually do?
faq.answer: It analyzes your request, picks the most relevant installed skills, and coordinates their guidance into one unified response instead of separate outputs.

faq.question: What are the three modes?
faq.answer: All related uses every relevant skill for broad coverage. Recommended picks the best balanced set. Restricted uses only the strongest one to three skills for focused output.

faq.question: Do I need to choose a mode every time?
faq.answer: Only when you invoke $use-skills without specifying a mode and no prior mode applies to your current task.

faq.question: Can this skill create new skills?
faq.answer: No, it only coordinates existing installed skills. It cannot create, modify, or install new skills.

faq.question: What happens if selected skills conflict?
faq.answer: The skill follows a priority order: user request first, session guidance second, narrower skills third, newer guidance fourth.

faq.question: How do I invoke this skill?
faq.answer: Type $use-skills or describe a task that clearly needs multiple skill areas like planning, implementation, and review.
